report_name: Huntress Uncovers Adware from Dragon Boss Solutions That Disabled AV and Exposed 25,000+ Systems to Trivial Supply Chain Attack

threat_actors.name: Dragon Boss Solutions
threat_actors.description: A UAE-based entity that digitally signed adware which disabled security products and created a significant supply chain vulnerability by using an unregistered domain for updates. The software was found on over 25,000 systems.
threat_actors.goals: Initial goal appears to be typical adware/PUP deployment, but its aggressive capabilities suggest a potential for more malicious follow-on activities.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: A PowerShell script with SYSTEM privileges was used to systematically disable a wide range of cybersecurity products by terminating their processes.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify System Firewall
attack_patterns.description: The malware modified the hosts file to block communication with update and telemetry servers of security products.

attack_patterns.name: Modify Registry
attack_patterns.description: The adware deleted registry keys associated with security products to prevent their reinstallation.

attack_patterns.name: Event Triggered Execution: Windows Management Instrumentation Event Subscription
attack_patterns.description: The malware established persistence through Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) event subscriptions to ensure it survived reboots.

attack_patterns.name: Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task
attack_patterns.description: The adware created scheduled tasks as a method of persistence.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: A PowerShell payload was executed with SYSTEM privileges to perform defense evasion and system modification tasks.

attack_patterns.name: Subvert Trust Controls: Code Signing
attack_patterns.description: The malicious adware was digitally signed by 'Dragon Boss Solutions' to appear as a legitimate program and bypass initial security checks.

malware.name: Dragon Boss Solutions Adware
malware.description: Adware signed by Dragon Boss Solutions that acted as a backdoor, disabling antivirus software, establishing persistence, and creating a supply chain vulnerability by attempting to fetch updates from an unregistered domain.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used with SYSTEM privileges to disable security products, block their update servers, and delete registry keys.

vulnerabilities.name: Dangling DNS Record for Update Domain
vulnerabilities.description: The adware attempted to fetch updates from the hardcoded domain 'chromsterabrowser.com', which was unregistered. This created a dangling domain vulnerability, allowing for a trivial supply chain hijack via domain takeover.

identities.name: Universities and colleges
identities.description: A class of victims affected by the Dragon Boss Solutions adware, with 221 identified institutions.

identities.name: Operational Technology (OT) networks
identities.description: A class of victims affected by the Dragon Boss Solutions adware, with 41 identified OT networks, including electric utilities.

identities.name: Government entities
identities.description: A class of victims affected by the Dragon Boss Solutions adware, with 35 identified government entities.

identities.name: Healthcare organizations
identities.description: A class of victims affected by the Dragon Boss Solutions adware, with 3 identified healthcare organizations.

infrastructure.name: Dragon Boss Adware Update Server
infrastructure.description: An update server infrastructure that the adware attempted to contact via the unregistered domain 'chromsterabrowser.com'. This created a major supply chain risk.

infrastructure.name: Dragon Boss Adware C2 Server
infrastructure.description: A command and control server associated with the campaign, using the domain 'worldwidewebframework3.com'.

indicators.name: Dragon Boss Solutions Adware C2/Update Domains
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Dragon Boss Solutions Adware C2 DNS Query
id: 7f5b1a3c-8d2e-4f9a-9c1b-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to domains associated with the Dragon Boss Solutions adware campaign.
references:
  - https://www.huntress.com/blog/pups-grow-fangs-dragon-boss-solutions-supply-chain-risk
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1071.004
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'chromsterabrowser.com'
      - 'worldwidewebframework3.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network connections to the C2 and update domains used by the Dragon Boss Solutions adware.

indicators.name: Dragon Boss Adware Disables Windows Defender via PowerShell
indicators.pattern:
title: Dragon Boss Adware Disables Windows Defender via PowerShell
id: 8a9b0c1d-2e3f-4a5b-6c7d-8e9f0a1b2c3d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a PowerShell command used by Dragon Boss Solutions adware to disable Windows Defender real-time monitoring.
references:
  - https://www.huntress.com/blog/pups-grow-fangs-dragon-boss-solutions-supply-chain-risk
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'Set-MpPreference'
      - '-DisableRealtimeMonitoring'
      - '$true'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the specific PowerShell command used by the adware to disable Windows Defender's real-time monitoring.

indicators.name: Dragon Boss Adware Hosts File Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: Dragon Boss Adware Hosts File Modification
id: 9b0c1d2e-3f4a-5b6c-7d8e-9f0a1b2c3d4e
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of the hosts file, a technique used by Dragon Boss Solutions adware to block security product updates.
references:
  - https://www.huntress.com/blog/pups-grow-fangs-dragon-boss-solutions-supply-chain-risk
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1562.004
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\drivers\etc\hosts'
  filter_legit:
    Image: 
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\notepad.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects modifications to the system's hosts file, a technique used by the adware to block security vendor communications.
